rulings.Case Title: ALBERT WRIGHT JR` et al VS. A.W. CHERSTON COMPANY, INC. et al

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 13, 2009, line 1, PLAINTIFF ALBERT WRIGHT, and, MARVA JOE WRIGHT Motion To Strike and Tax Memorandum of Costs of Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corporation. Motion to tax costs granted as to one-half the deposition costs only, pursuant to counsels' agreement. Defendant awarded costs in the amountof $2,557.65. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN GIDDING et al VS. IVAN KAYE et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 13, 2009, line 3, DEFENDANT BRIANNE FISHER, PARITUA VINEYARDS LTD., FISHER & COMPANY, INC. MOTION TO STRIKE. Motion granted without leave to amend, but without prejudice to plaintiffs filing a motion to amend. San Francisco Superior Court requires leave of the Court for a Doe amendment. Defendants have right to bring this motion because the substitution of new parties will adversely affect their rights. New discovery will likely occur and another trial continuance may be necessary. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: GATEWAY BUSINESS BANK, A CALIFORNIA COLLECTION et al VS. VILLA DEI FIORI CONDOMINIUMS L.P. A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 13, 2009, line 4, CROSS DEFENDANT GATEWAY BUSINESS BANK, DEMURRER TO THIRD AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained, ten days leave to amend. Cross-complainants to plead specific terms of contract, including Gateway's relationship to cross-complainants, Gateway's duty to cross-complaints, and breach under the contract. If the seventh cause of action is based upon the Trustee's Deed Upon Sale or any agreement, the document should be attached to the contract or its essential terms pleaded in the cross-complaint. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES A SHOTWELL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A PUBLIC ENTITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 13, 2009, line 6, PLAINTIFF JAMES SHOTWELL Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. Motion for judgment on the pleadings granted, thirty days leave to amend. Defendant must plead facts supporting its affirmative defenses. (CCP section 438(c)(1)(A) and 438(c)(2)(B); FPI Development, Inc. vs. Nakashima (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 367, 384.) =(302/CWW)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 13, 2009, line 10, DEFENDANT CHIA NEI DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Off Calendar. Amended Complaint filed. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: BARRY LEE VS. CHIA JI NEI et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 13, 2009, line 10, DEFENDANT CHIA NEI DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to the first cause of action for nonpayment of rent. Notice fatally defective under CCP section 1161(2) because Winata's telephone number is not provided. Notice does not clearly state that Barry Lee is authorized to receive rent. Unlawful detainer notices are strictly construed. Baugh vs.Consumers Associates, Ltd. (1966) 241 Cal.App.2nd 672. Demurrer overruled as to second cause of action for failure to pay taxes. That cure or quit notice is based upon CCP section 1161(3) and therefore, the requirements of CCP section 1161(2) are inapplicable. The complaint is sufficiently certain. Defendant to answer within five days. =(302/CWW)
